name: Leiothrichidae
description: Leiothrichidae is a large and diverse family of passerine birds found primarily in Asia. They include the laughingthrushes, mesias, minlas, and sibias, known for their often colorful plumage and loud, musical calls.

children.name: Grammatoptila
children.description: A monotypic genus of laughingthrush with boldly striped plumage, found in the Himalayas.

children.name: Pterorhinus
children.description: A genus of medium to large laughingthrushes with varied plumage, found across East and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Pterorhinus pectoralis
children.description: #REDIRECT Greater necklaced laughingthrush.

children.name: Pterorhinus monileger
children.description: A laughingthrush with a narrow black necklace, grey upperparts, and white underparts. It ranges from the Himalayas through Southeast Asia to southern China.

children.name: Pterorhinus nigrimentum
children.description: A laughingthrush with a black chin, white face, chestnut crown, and olive-brown body. It inhabits montane forests of southern China and northern Vietnam.

children.name: Garrulax
children.description: A genus of striking laughingthrushes with bold head patterns, found in Asia.

children.name: Garrulax ferrugineus
children.description: A laughingthrush with a rufous forehead and crown, grey face, and olive-brown body. It inhabits montane forests of the eastern Himalayas.

children.name: Garrulax leucogaster
children.description: A laughingthrush with a white belly, chestnut crown, black mask, and grey upperparts. It is found in the mountains of southern India.

children.name: Garrulax maurus
children.description: A laughingthrush with a chestnut back and wings, white face, black mask, and grey underparts. It is endemic to the mountains of Sumatra.

children.name: Ianthocincla
children.description: A genus of colourful laughingthrushes with distinctive facial patterns and spotted or ocellated plumage. They are found in montane forests of the Himalayas and China.

children.name: Leiothrix
children.description: A genus of brightly colored, small to medium-sized birds with melodious songs, found in Asia.

children.name: Actinodura
children.description: A genus of small to medium-sized birds including minlas and barwings, found in montane forests of Asia.

children.name: Actinodura strigula
children.description: #REDIRECT Bar-throated minla {{Redirect category shell| }}.

children.name: Minla
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Red-tailed Minla, a colourful bird of montane forests in East Asia.

children.name: Trochalopteron
children.description: A genus of colorful laughingthrushes with scaled or patterned plumage, found in montane forests of Asia.

children.name: Trochalopteron ocellatum
children.description: The Spotted Laughingthrush has dark plumage with prominent white spots on the breast and head, and rufous-tinged wings. It inhabits rhododendron and oak forests in the Himalayas.

children.name: Trochalopteron erythrocephalum
children.description: #REDIRECT Chestnut-crowned laughingthrush.

children.name: Trochalopteron ludlowi
children.description: A laughingthrush with a grey crown, black mask, olive-brown body, and rufous wings. It is found in the mountains of northeastern India and adjacent Tibet.

children.name: Heterophasia
children.description: A genus of slender, long-tailed birds with melodious songs, known as sibias, found in montane forests of Asia.

children.name: Laniellus
children.description: A small genus of strikingly patterned birds with spotted and streaked plumage, found in montane forests of Southeast Asia. They are related to the sibias and have long, graduated tails.

children.name: Cutia
children.description: A genus of strikingly patterned birds with barred underparts, found in montane forests of Asia.

children.name: Monteincila
children.description: A small genus of laughingthrushes with rufous and grey plumage, found in the mountains of Southeast Asia.

children.name: Monteincila merulina
children.description: A laughingthrush with a white throat and breast, chestnut crown, and olive-brown upperparts. It is found in montane forests of Sumatra and the Malay Peninsula.

children.name: Leucodioptron
children.description: A genus of laughingthrushes with distinctive white or red markings on the face, found in the mountains of East and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Turdoides
children.description: A genus of medium-sized laughingthrushes with plain or streaked brown plumage, found in scrub and open woodlands of Asia.

children.name: Turdoides malcomi
children.description: A large grey babbler with a pale yellow eye, white throat, and long tail. It is found in dry scrub and open woodlands of the Indian subcontinent.

children.name: Argya
children.description: A genus of medium to large babblers with plain or streaked plumage, found in scrub and grasslands of Asia. They are highly social, often moving in noisy flocks through their habitat.

children.name: Argya caranulata
children.description: A small babbler with tawny underparts, streaked brown upperparts, and a yellow eye. It is found in scrub and grasslands of southern India and Sri Lanka.

children.name: Argya cinereiceps
children.description: A babbler with a grey head, brown upperparts, and buffy underparts. It is endemic to the Western Ghats of India.

children.name: Chatarrhaea
children.description: A small genus of babblers found in scrub and grasslands of Asia, with plain brown or grey plumage and long tails.

children.name: Chatarrhaea earlei
children.description: A babbler with heavy pale streaking on the brown upperparts, white underparts, and a long tail. It is found in reed beds and grasslands of the Indus and Ganges river systems.

children.name: Acroptila
children.description: A genus of babblers found in scrub and forests of Asia, with plain or slightly streaked plumage.

children.name: Acroptila albiventris
children.description: A small babbler with a white belly, grey head, brown upperparts, and a short tail. It is endemic to the hills of southern India and Sri Lanka.

children.name: Lanioplus
children.description: A genus of small babblers with rufous and white plumage, found in the Himalayas and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Lanioplus rufiscapilla
children.description: A small babbler with a rufous crown, white underparts, brown upperparts, and a long tail. It is found in scrub and forests of the Indian subcontinent.

children.name: Grammatopia
children.description: A genus of babblers with bold streaking on the head and breast, found in the Himalayas and China.

children.name: Grammatopia striata
children.description: A babbler with heavy white streaking on the brown head and breast, brown upperparts, and a long tail. It is found in scrub and grasslands of the Himalayas and southern China.

children.name: Melanocichla
children.description: A small genus of bold black-and-white laughingthrushes with stout, curved bills. They are found in limestone karst forests of Southeast Asia and are known for their loud, ringing calls.

children.name: Melanocichla macrodactyla
children.description: A large black babbler with a white head and throat, a stout curved bill, and a long tail. It is found in limestone karst forests of Thailand, Myanmar, and Indochina.

children.name: Sibia
children.description: A genus of long-tailed, melodious birds found in montane forests of Asia. They forage actively in the canopy, often in mixed-species flocks, and are known for their clear, whistled songs.

children.name: Sibia nigriceps
children.description: A sibia with a black cap, blue-grey upperparts, chestnut tail and underparts, and white wing patches. It is found in montane forests of the Himalayas.

children.name: Sibia leucocephala
children.description: A sibia with a white crown, black face, blue-grey upperparts, and chestnut underparts. It is endemic to the mountains of Borneo.

children.name: Leioptila
children.description: A monotypic genus of sibia with a rufous back and black-and-white head pattern, found in the eastern Himalayas.

children.name: Malacocincla
children.description: A genus of small, short-tailed babblers with rufous and grey plumage, found in forests of Southeast Asia. They are shy, secretive birds that forage on the forest floor.

children.name: Malacocincla aspera
children.description: A babbler with a rough, spiky crest, brown upperparts, and pale underparts. It is found in lowland forests of Thailand and Myanmar.

children.name: Pellorneum
children.description: A genus of ground-dwelling babblers with streaked or spotted plumage, found in forests of Asia. They are secretive birds that forage in leaf litter, often cocking their tails upward.

children.name: Pellorneum nigriceps
children.description: A babbler with a black crown, white face, brown upperparts, and white underparts. It is endemic to the forests of Borneo.

children.name: Trichastoma
children.description: A genus of medium-sized babblers with plain brown plumage and long tails, found in forests of Southeast Asia.

children.name: Kenopia
children.description: A monotypic genus of babbler with distinctive black-and-white facial patterns and a preference for lowland forests of Southeast Asia.

children.name: Napothera
children.description: A genus of small, short-tailed babblers with rufous and grey plumage, found in montane forests of Asia. They are often difficult to observe as they forage in dense undergrowth.

children.name: Napothera crassior
children.description: A small babbler with rich brown upperparts, white throat, and rufous underparts. It is endemic to the mountains of Borneo.

children.name: Napothera rajah
children.description: A small babbler with brown upperparts, white underparts heavily scaled with black, and a white supercilium. It is found in montane forests of Borneo.

children.name: Stachyris
children.description: A genus of medium-sized babblers with rufous, grey, and olive plumage, found in forests of Asia. They are active, social birds that forage in noisy flocks in the undergrowth.

children.name: Dumeticola
children.description: A genus of small, secretive babblers with streaked brown plumage, found in dense grasslands and scrub of Asia.

children.name: Dumeticola thoracica
children.description: A small babbler with streaked brown upperparts, white underparts spotted with black, and a white supercilium. It is found in grasslands of the eastern Himalayas and China.

children.name: Dumeticola davidi
children.description: A small babbler with brown upperparts, pale underparts, and a prominent white supercilium. It is found in reed beds and grasslands of East Asia.

children.name: Dumeticola striata
children.description: A babbler with heavily streaked upperparts, white underparts, and a long tail. It is found in grasslands and scrub of the Himalayas and southern China.

children.name: Tephrodornis
children.description: A genus of sturdy, thick-billed birds with grey and brown plumage, found in forests of Asia. They are sometimes placed in their own family but are now included in the laughingthrush family.

children.name: Erophila
children.description: A genus of colourful laughingthrushes with bright rufous or chestnut plumage, found in the mountains of China and the Himalayas.

children.name: Erophila erythrocephala
children.description: A laughingthrush with a chestnut crown, black mask, and olive-brown body. It is found in montane forests of the eastern Himalayas.

children.name: Erophila rufogularis
children.description: A laughingthrush with a rufous throat, grey crown, and olive-brown body. It is found in montane forests of the Himalayas.

children.name: Cinclosoma
children.description: A genus of terrestrial birds with subtly patterned plumage, found in arid and semi-arid regions of Australia. They are shy, ground-dwelling birds that run rather than fly when disturbed.

children.name: Cinclosoma castaneum
children.description: A quail-thrush with a rich chestnut back, black-and-white facial pattern, and pale underparts. It is found in arid scrublands of central Australia.

children.name: Cinclosoma albogularis
children.description: A quail-thrush with a white throat, brown upperparts spotted with black, and grey underparts. It is found in dry forests of northern Australia.

children.name: Psophodes
children.description: A genus of Australian birds with distinctive, loud calls that often resemble whipcracks or whistles. They are ground-foraging birds with long, cocked tails and strong legs.

children.name: Psophodes crepitans
children.description: A secretive bird with a black crest and breast, olive-green body, and a long tail. It is famous for its whip-crack song, often sung as a duet.

children.name: Sphenostoma
children.description: A monotypic genus of Australian bird with a distinctive wedge-shaped bill and loud, whistling calls. It inhabits arid and semi-arid scrublands.

children.name: Sphenostoma cristatum
children.description: A bird with a short crest, grey-brown upperparts, white throat, and black breast band. Its far-carrying, bell-like song is a characteristic sound of the Australian outback.

children.name: Coracina
children.description: A large genus of medium-sized birds with grey, black, and white plumage, found across Asia and Australasia. They are arboreal insectivores with a hooked bill.

children.name: Coracina melano
children.description: A cuckooshrike with grey upperparts, white underparts, and black wings. It is found in forests of South and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Coracina novaeguineae
children.description: A cuckooshrike with grey upperparts, white underparts, and black wings. It is found in forests of New Guinea.

children.name: Coracina atakamensis
children.description: A cuckooshrike with dark grey plumage and a black mask. It is found in montane forests of Southeast Asia.

children.name: Pomatostomus
children.description: A genus of medium-sized, social birds with curved bills and brown and white plumage, found in Australia and New Guinea. They build large, dome-shaped communal nests.

children.name: Garrodia
children.description: A small genus of Asian babblers with plain brown plumage, found in forests of the eastern Himalayas and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Garrodia subtilis
children.description: A plain brown babbler with a pale throat and belly, and a long tail. It is found in forests of the eastern Himalayas.

children.name: Garrodia leucocrypta
children.description: A babbler with a white breast and throat, brown upperparts, and a long tail. It is found in forests of Southeast Asia.
